text: The video shows a contracted gallbladder encased in a layer of surrounding fat, as observed during the procedure.

I'll switch to indesigning green imaging here. And again, as standard fashion for acute chole's, the cystic duct doesn't really light up. Be indesigning green. Acute Coley's, I'll usually use three cc's to four cc's, sometimes a little bit more for acute ones, just to hopefully that it lights up. I try to give it between thirty and forty-five minutes before and holding.

The surgeon uses four robotic arms to dissect the cystic artery, aiming to achieve the critical view of safety. Arm one holds a ProGrasp, arm two a Maryland Dissector, arm three the camera, and arm four a hook cautery. The cystic artery is isolated with sufficient space for three clips, and the margin of the liver is visible.

The surgeon places clips on the cystic artery, resulting in minor bleeding that is quickly controlled with cautery. The procedure progresses towards the cystic plate in a standard robotic cholecystectomy.
